SEARCH
Package dev.jeka.core.api.file

Class JkPathSequence

java.lang.Object
dev.jeka.core.api.file.JkPathSequence
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable, Iterable<Path>
public final class JkPathSequence extends Object implements Iterable<Path>, Serializable
A sequence of file path (folder or archive). Each file is called an entry.
Instances of this class are immutable.
See Also:
  • Serialized Form

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    JkPathSequence
    and(Iterable<Path> otherEntries)
    Returns a JkPathSequence made of, in the order, the entries of this one plus the specified ones.

    Creates a JkPathSequence from a base directory and string of relative paths separated with a ";".

    Creates a JkPathSequence form specified entries

    static JkPathSequence
    ofSysPropClassPath()
    Returns the current classpath as given by System.getProperty("java.class.path").
    static JkPathSequence
    readFrom(Path path)
    Reads the contents of a file at the given path and returns a JkPathSequence object representing the paths extracted from the file contents.
    static JkPathSequence
    readFromQuietly(Path path)
    Same as readFrom(java.nio.file.Path) but returns an empty invalid input: 'if the file does not exist.'
    JkPathSequence
    relativizeFromWorkingDir()
    Returns an identical path sequence but replacing relative paths with absolute paths resolved from the specified base directory.
    JkPathSequence
    resolvedTo(Path baseDir)
    Returns an identical path sequence but replacing relative paths with absolute paths resolved from the specified base directory.
    String
    toColonSeparatedPath()
    Returns the file names concatenated with ':'

    String
    toPath()
    Returns the file names concatenated with ';' on Windows and ':' on unix.

  • Method Details

    • of

      public static JkPathSequence of(Iterable<Path> paths)
      Creates a JkPathSequence from an Iterable of paths or a single Path.
      Parameters:
      paths - As Path class implements { @link Iterable } the argument can be a single Path instance, if so it will be interpreted as a list containing a single element which is this argument.

    • of

      public static JkPathSequence of()

    • readFrom

      public static JkPathSequence readFrom(Path path)
      Reads the contents of a file at the given path and returns a JkPathSequence object representing the paths extracted from the file contents. The file content is supposed to be have been written using writeTo(java.nio.file.Path)

      Throws:
      UncheckedIOException - if the file does not exist.

    • readFromQuietly

      public static JkPathSequence readFromQuietly(Path path)
      Same as readFrom(java.nio.file.Path) but returns an empty invalid input: 'if the file does not exist.'

    • ofPathString

      public static JkPathSequence ofPathString(String pathString)

    • of

      public static JkPathSequence of(Path baseDir, String relativePathsAsString)
      Creates a JkPathSequence from a base directory and string of relative paths separated with a ";".

    • of

      public static JkPathSequence of(Path path1, Path path2, Path... others)
      Creates a JkPathSequence form specified entries

    • ofSysPropClassPath

      public static JkPathSequence ofSysPropClassPath()
      Returns the current classpath as given by System.getProperty("java.class.path").

    • withoutDuplicates

      public JkPathSequence withoutDuplicates()
      Returns a JkPathSequence identical to this one but without duplicates. If a given file in this sequence exist twice or more, then only the first occurrence is kept in the returned sequence.

    • normalized

      public JkPathSequence normalized()

    • getEntries

      @Deprecated public List<Path> getEntries()
      Deprecated.
      Use toList() instead.
      Returns this sequence as a list.

    • toList

      public List<Path> toList()

      public Path getElement(int index)

      public boolean isEmpty()

      @Deprecated public Path getEntry(int index)

      Use getElement(int) instead.

      Specified by:
      iterator in interface Iterable<Path>

    • andPrepend

      public JkPathSequence andPrepend(Path path1, Path path2, Path... entries)
      See Also:
      • andPrepend(Iterable)

    • andPrepend

      public JkPathSequence andPrepend(Iterable<Path> paths)
      Returns a JkPathSequence made of the specified entries followed by the sequence entries of this object.
      Parameters:
      paths - As Path class implements { @link Iterable } the argument can be a single Path instance, if so it will be interpreted as a list containing a single element which is this argument.

    • and

      public JkPathSequence and(Path path1, Path path2, Path... others)
      See Also:
      • and(Iterable)

    • and

      public JkPathSequence and(Iterable<Path> otherEntries)
      Returns a JkPathSequence made of, in the order, the entries of this one plus the specified ones.

    • resolvedTo

      public JkPathSequence resolvedTo(Path baseDir)
      Returns an identical path sequence but replacing relative paths with absolute paths resolved from the specified base directory.

    • relativizeFromWorkingDir

      public JkPathSequence relativizeFromWorkingDir()
      Returns an identical path sequence but replacing relative paths with absolute paths resolved from the specified base directory.

    • toPath

      public String toPath()
      Returns the file names concatenated with ';' on Windows and ':' on unix.

    • toColonSeparatedPath

      public String toColonSeparatedPath()
      Returns the file names concatenated with ':'

    • toPathMultiLine

      public String toPathMultiLine(String margin)

      Returns the first entry of this classpath containing the given class.
